_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Python Runtime
author: parker@parkervcp.com
description: Provision a Python runtime with pip support and standard tooling for PetroPanel code workloads.
startup: if [[ -d .git ]] && [[ "{{AUTO_UPDATE}}" == "1" ]]; then git pull; fi; if [[ ! -z "{{PY_PACKAGES}}" ]]; then pip install -U --prefix .local {{PY_PACKAGES}}; fi; if [[ -f /home/container/${REQUIREMENTS_FILE} ]]; then pip install -U --prefix .local -r ${REQUIREMENTS_FILE}; fi; /usr/local/bin/python /home/container/{{PY_FILE}}

installation.script:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
IFS=$'\n\t'

BRAND="BrickHosting.org"
SERVER_DIR="/mnt/server"

log() { echo "[${BRAND}] $*"; }
fail() { echo "[${BRAND}][error] $*" >&2; exit 1; }
trap 'fail "Failed on line ${LINENO}"' ERR

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

log "Starting Python install"

mkdir -p "${SERVER_DIR}"
cd "${SERVER_DIR}" || fail "Cannot access ${SERVER_DIR}"
export HOME="${SERVER_DIR}"

apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
  git curl jq file unzip make gcc g++ libtool ca-certificates

# user upload mode
if [[ "${USER_UPLOAD:-0}" == "true" || "${USER_UPLOAD:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
  log "User upload mode active"
  exit 0
fi

[[ -z "${GIT_ADDRESS:-}" ]] && fail "GIT_ADDRESS missing"

# normalize repo url
[[ "${GIT_ADDRESS}" != *.git ]] && GIT_ADDRESS="${GIT_ADDRESS}.git"

# auth support
if [[ -n "${USERNAME:-}" && -n "${ACCESS_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then
  GIT_ADDRESS="https://${USERNAME}:${ACCESS_TOKEN}@$(echo "${GIT_ADDRESS}" | cut -d/ -f3-)"
fi

# repo handling
if [[ -n "$(ls -A "${SERVER_DIR}")" ]]; then
  if [[ -d .git ]]; then
    ORIGIN="$(git config --get remote.origin.url || true)"
    if [[ "${ORIGIN}" == "${GIT_ADDRESS}" ]]; then
      log "Updating repository"
      git pull --ff-only
    else
      log "Origin mismatch, skipping pull"
    fi
  fi
else
  log "Cloning repository"
  if [[ -n "${BRANCH:-}" ]]; then
    git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --branch "${BRANCH}" "${GIT_ADDRESS}" .
  else
    git clone --depth 1 "${GIT_ADDRESS}" .
  fi
fi

command -v pip >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "pip not found"

# extra packages
if [[ -n "${PY_PACKAGES:-}" ]]; then
  pip install --no-cache-dir -U --prefix .local ${PY_PACKAGES}
fi

REQ_FILE="${REQUIREMENTS_FILE:-requirements.txt}"
if [[ -f "${REQ_FILE}" ]]; then
  pip install --no-cache-dir -U --prefix .local -r "${REQ_FILE}"
fi

log "Ready"
exit 0

variables.name: Git Repo Address
variables.description:
Git repo to clone

I.E. https://github.com/parkervcp/repo_name

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description:
What branch to pull from github.

Default is blank to pull the repo default branch

variables.name: User Uploaded Files
variables.description:
Skip all the install stuff if you are letting a user upload files.

0 = false (default)
1 = true

variables.name: Auto Update
variables.description: Pull the latest files on startup when using a GitHub repo.

variables.name: App py file
variables.description: The file that starts the App.

variables.name: Additional Python packages
variables.description:
Install additional python packages.

Use spaces to separate

variables.name: Git Access Token
variables.description:
Password to use with git.

It's best practice to use a Personal Access Token.
https://github.com/settings/tokens
https://gitlab.com/-/profile/personal_access_tokens

variables.name: Requirements file
variables.description: if there are other requirements files to choose from.
